faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Bellerose home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small irregular holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery; silken tubes or cases in closets; adult moths flying near wardrobes; and webbing or cocoons in fabric folds. You may also notice larvae (small caterpillars) on clothing or shed skins in affected areas.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Bellerose?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Bellerose typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of treatment areas required. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment reduces adult moth populations immediately, but complete elimination of larvae and eggs typically requires 4-8 weeks with follow-up treatments. Our multi-visit programs ensure all life stages are addressed, and we provide guidance on cleaning and prevention to accelerate results.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths damage more than just clothing?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths feed on natural fibers including wool, silk, fur, feathers, and cashmere found in carpets, upholstered furniture, drapes, blankets, and even taxidermy. They can cause extensive damage to valuable textiles, antiques, and household items if left untreated.
